S&P Global Market Intelligence, 2025
42%

of companies abandoned AI initiatives in 2025

33x

POCs launched for every 4 that reach production

The Problem

Your AI initiative is probably in trouble. Here's why.

Board pressure creates pilots born from FOMO, not ROI. Technical teams optimise F1-scores while integration tasks sit untouched. Nobody's talking to compliance, IT, or the people who'll actually use the thing.

By the time someone asks "when do we go live?" — the integration challenges, data governance issues, and user resistance surface all at once. The project gets quietly deprioritised. Sound familiar?

The 5–30% of AI projects that succeed share one thing: they treat implementation as an organisational challenge, not a technical one.

Read the full analysis →

What We Do

End-to-end AI delivery

From identifying the right problems to embedding AI into how your team actually works.

⚙️

AI Implementation

We take your highest-value use cases from POC to production — with integrations, governance, and change management built in from day one.

🎯

AI Strategy & Consulting

Identify which AI bets will move your P&L. We audit your data readiness, prioritise use cases, and build a roadmap leadership actually believes in.

🧠

Capability Building

Upskill your teams to sustain AI initiatives long after the engagement ends. Custom programs for executives, PMs, and practitioners.

Built for scale

Platforms that embed learning and AI capability directly into how your organisation works.

Why SnapSkill

We've seen why pilots fail.
We know how to prevent it.

📊

Business-first thinking

Every engagement starts with the P&L impact, not the model architecture.

🔗

Integration by default

We design for your legacy systems, compliance stack, and real users from day one.

📈

Outcomes, not outputs

We track business KPIs — revenue, cost, time — not just technical accuracy metrics.

🤝

Knowledge transfer

Your teams own the AI, not us. We build internal capability as we deliver.

Ready to move from pilot to production?

Let's start with an honest conversation about where your AI initiative stands — and what it would take to make it work.
